policy_statement: Communicate an updated NDC every 5 years that progresses beyond the current NDC and reflects highest possible ambition, informed by the global stocktake.
mechanism_description: Communicating an updated NDC every five years, informed by the global stocktake, increases mitigation ambition over time and supports an emissions-reduction pathway.
primary_causal_pathway: Governance target-setting (NDC update/ratchet) → stronger mitigation policies/implementation → ↓ GHG emissions → ↓ atmospheric forcing
causal_mechanism_detail:
1) Change in human activity/system: The government institutionalizes a periodic (e.g., 5-year) cycle to review, update, and communicate NDC targets and associated plans, informed by the global stocktake.  
2) Mediating physical/process mechanism: Higher ambition targets and clearer implementation signals drive adoption/strengthening of emissions-reducing measures (e.g., energy transition, efficiency, land-use measures), changing economy-wide activity and technology choices that determine fuel combustion and other emission sources.  
3) Climate-relevant outcome: Reduced national GHG emissions (and potentially increased removals), contributing to lower cumulative emissions and reduced radiative forcing relative to a no-ratchet baseline.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is that repeated NDC updates ratchet up mitigation ambition, leading to lower GHG emissions than would occur without the cycle.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: A mandated NDC update cycle increases mitigation ambition and triggers stronger emissions-reduction actions, yielding ↓ GHG emissions.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: While the mechanism is procedural (governance), its principal intended and typical climate effect is mitigation via emissions reductions; it is not primarily about reducing hazard exposure (adaptation), reducing resource inputs for the same service (resource efficiency as the main effect), or ecosystem-based carbon sinks (nature-based solutions).  
3) Edge cases applied: Some NDC updates include adaptation components, finance, or capacity-building, but the stated mechanism here is explicitly “increased mitigation ambition and emissions-reduction pathway,” so mitigation remains dominant.  
4) Applies to ALL instances sharing this mechanism: Any policy framed as periodic NDC updating to “progress/ratchet ambition” is fundamentally aimed at strengthening mitigation commitments, with emissions reductions as the largest climate-relevant effect.  
5) Secondary subtype assignment deferred: Any further labeling (e.g., economy-wide mitigation planning, transparency/MRV, sectoral mitigation focus) requires policy-specific evidence and is not assigned at the mechanism level.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: If an “NDC update cycle” policy is implemented purely as a reporting/communication exercise without translating into stronger targets or measures, its real-world mitigation impact could be weak; however, the canonical mechanism definition assumes ratcheting ambition leading to emissions reductions. Also, NDCs can bundle adaptation targets, but those are secondary unless explicitly dominant.
key_indicators: "be informed by the global stocktake"; "communicate a NDC every 5 years"; "represents a progression beyond"; "reflects ... highest possible ambition"
instance_edge_case_notes: While “communicate” could be read as an administrative/reporting obligation, the substance is the ratcheting of NDC ambition; classified as target-setting (not information-disclosure) because the primary function is to set/raise mitigation targets rather than merely disclose information.
